comment: Top-level requires are evaluated eagerly when the module loads. Consider lazy-loading ui, picker, and actions inside the functions that use them to avoid loading nui.nvim and snacks upfront.
json_pretty shells out to python via :%! which can fail silently if python is not on PATH. Consider a pure-Lua JSON formatter or at minimum check the exit code.

Buffer-local <CR> keymap overrides the default Enter behavior for the entire buffer. This breaks normal editing. Consider using a different keymap like <leader>ra or only binding when cursor is on a reviewed line.

wrap_text breaks at the last whitespace within width, but does not handle words longer than width (e.g. long URLs). These would loop infinitely. Add a fallback break at width when no whitespace is found.
The header line construction is hard to follow with inline concatenation and fill math. Consider extracting a build_header helper for clarity.
vim.fs.joinpath uses the OS separator on Windows which may produce backslash paths. Snacks icon lookup might not handle mixed separators. Normalize with gsub after joining.
The nui.Menu has no keymap hint or footer. Users won't know they can press j/k to navigate and Enter to select. Add a bottom border text with a short hint.
